quotes.text: If your onboarding needs a tutorial, your onboarding has failed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best onboarding is invisible. The user shouldn't even know they're being onboarded.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your product has a 'getting started' guide that's 47 pages long, your product is too complicated.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The hardest part of SaaS isn't acquisition. It's activation. Getting someone to sign up is easy. Getting them to use it is hard.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every SaaS should have a 'quick start' that takes 5 minutes. If your quick start takes 30 minutes, you've lost everyone.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your onboarding email sequence should feel like a helpful friend, not a desperate salesperson.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best SaaS onboarding is no onboarding. The product should explain itself.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to improve your product's onboarding is to watch 5 new users try to use it. Record their screen. Take notes.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best SaaS onboarding takes 3 steps or less. If it takes more, simplify the product.
quotes.author: based
